	{"bbox_2d": [72, 122, 109, 133], "text_content": "2.2.8"},
	{"bbox_2d": [164, 121, 391, 133], "text_content": "POWER_OK2 / POWER_OK3"},
	{"bbox_2d": [164, 137, 847, 149], "text_content": "These pins report by default the status of the USB power delivery contract negotiation with the source."},
	{"bbox_2d": [164, 152, 881, 164], "text_content": "Different configurations are proposed as stated in the table below to meet specific application requirements."},
	{"bbox_2d": [164, 167, 879, 193], "text_content": "The configuration of the POWER_OK pins can be changed by NVM programming (see Section 5 Start-up configuration)."},
	{"bbox_2d": [164, 207, 927, 220], "text_content": "Depending on the programmed configuration, they can be used in combination with VBUS_EN_SNK pin to enable"},
	{"bbox_2d": [164, 222, 300, 234], "text_content": "different power path scenarios."},
	{"bbox_2d": [164, 237, 927, 249], "text_content": "POWER_OK2 pin is a high voltage open drain output that allows a PMOS transistor to be directly driven to enable"},
	{"bbox_2d": [164, 252, 275, 264], "text_content": "a power path."},
	{"bbox_2d": [164, 254, 494, 266], "text_content": "POWER_OK3 is a low voltage open drain output."},
